summary.narrative: Mayor Jerry Dyer is serving his second term after winning re-election in March 2024. His administration has centered on his 'One Fresno' vision emphasizing inclusivity, neighborhood pride, and responsive government. Insufficient verified data exists to fully assess promise completion status; further investigation is needed to assign definitive grades.

promises.text: Create an inclusive, prosperous city through the 'One Fresno' vision
promises.evidence: Dyer has consistently promoted the One Fresno initiative throughout his tenure, with ongoing equity programs and community engagement efforts, though measurable outcomes require further verification.

promises.text: Reduce homelessness in Fresno through increased services and housing initiatives
promises.evidence: The Dyer administration has invested in homeless services and navigation centers, but homelessness remains a significant challenge in Fresno with mixed results on reducing unsheltered populations.

promises.text: Improve public safety and reduce crime rates citywide
promises.evidence: As a former police chief, Dyer has prioritized public safety funding and police recruitment, though crime statistics show varied results across different categories.

promises.text: Beautify neighborhoods and reduce blight throughout Fresno
promises.evidence: City has expanded clean-up programs and code enforcement efforts as part of the beautification initiative, with ongoing neighborhood improvement projects.

promises.text: Create a government that listens and keeps its promises to residents
promises.evidence: Dyer has held community meetings and town halls, and the city website explicitly references promise-keeping as a core value, but systematic tracking of commitments is limited.

promises.text: Promote economic development and job creation in Fresno
promises.evidence: Administration has pursued business attraction and downtown revitalization efforts, with 2025 plans including continued economic development initiatives.
